digest.component_or_area: rebar documentation remediation and docs-drift CI gates
propositions: README session-log command is invalid (missing the append subcommand) and the quickstart is not a true end-to-end single-ticket flow.
Dependency count and mcp version floor are wrong in README and docs/config.md (truth: 3 base deps, mcp>=1.9).
docs/output-schemas.md points at a moved source path and describes a removed bash shell-out; stale bash-engine language remains in CI comments and leaf.py.
docs/README.md index is incomplete (unlinked living docs) and mixes living reference with historical evidence.
A CI gate must extract and execute the literal README quickstart fenced commands so printed-command text rot fails the build.
A docs-index CI gate must fail the build when living docs are unlinked or relative links (including those relocated under docs/archive/) do not resolve.
